decisions.title: Why Non-Blocking Execution
decisions.description: Background tasks run in separate threads or processes, allowing the main agent loop to continue handling user input. Without this, a long-running task (like running a test suite or building a project) would freeze the agent until completion. Non-blocking execution lets the agent work on other tasks, check progress, or respond to the user while background work proceeds.
decisions.alternatives: Sequential execution is simpler and avoids concurrency issues, but it wastes time when the agent could be productive during a wait. Async/await patterns work within a single thread but require restructuring all tool functions. Separate threads/processes provide isolation with minimal code changes.

zh.title: 为什么需要非阻塞执行
zh.description: 后台任务在独立的线程或进程中运行，使主 Agent 循环能继续处理用户输入。如果没有这个机制，一个长时间运行的任务（如执行测试套件或构建项目）会让 Agent 完全冻结直到完成。非阻塞执行让 Agent 在后台工作进行的同时，还能处理其他任务、检查进度或回复用户。

decisions.title: Why Notification Queue over Polling
decisions.description: When a background task completes, it pushes a notification to a queue that the agent checks at the start of each turn. This is more efficient than polling: the agent does not waste API calls asking 'is it done yet?' repeatedly. The notification carries the task result, so the agent can immediately act on the outcome without an additional lookup.
decisions.alternatives: Polling at fixed intervals is simple but wasteful — each poll consumes tokens and API calls. Callback-based interruption would be ideal but is hard to implement safely in a turn-based conversation model. A notification queue checked once per turn is the pragmatic middle ground.

zh.title: 为什么用通知队列而非轮询
zh.description: 当后台任务完成时，它会将通知推送到一个队列中，Agent 在每轮对话开始时检查该队列。这比轮询更高效：Agent 不需要反复浪费 API 调用来询问「完成了吗？」。通知携带了任务结果，Agent 可以立即根据结果采取行动，无需额外查询。
